Graduation Project

Graduation Project

Designed and built a fully autonomous Eco-Plastic Tile Machine that converts recycled plastic waste and desert sand into structural interlocking tiles — aligned with Egypt's Vision 2030. I was responsible for the full Electrical & Automation System: PLC programming (Wecon), VFD control, temperature PID loops, hydraulic sequencing, sensor integration, and EPLAN electrical documentation.

🎓 Graduation Project

MYM — Plastic-to-Tiles Machine

Co-Founded · Egypt Vision 2030 · Mechatronics Engineering

Mechatronics Startup Co-Founder Vision 2030 Plastic Recycling Industrial Machine Design
🏆
Grade
Excellent
🏭
Machine Type
Eco-Plastic Tile Machine
♻️
Output
Plastic–Sand Interlocking Tiles

What is MYM?

MYM is a fully autonomous mechatronics system designed to transform post-consumer plastic waste and desert sand into high-durability interlocking tiles — with zero human intervention. It directly addresses Egypt's plastic pollution crisis while producing a sustainable alternative to conventional cement-based construction materials.

The project is aligned with Egypt's Vision 2030 and the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDG 9 — Industry & Innovation, SDG 12 — Responsible Consumption), and was graded Excellent by Delta Technological University.

⚠️ Problem Statement

Current plastic recycling methods rely on hazardous manual processing or primitive semi-automated tools — lacking thermal precision, uniform mixing, and consistent compaction forces. The result: defective products, unsafe environments, and complete dependence on imported machinery. MYM solves this with a 100% closed-loop automated sequence.

How It Works — The 4-Stage Process

01 Gravity-Fed Material Intake

A hopper receives granulated plastic waste. A screw-driven feedstock dispensing unit precisely controls the material feed rate into the thermal processing zone.

02 Thermal Fusion & Extrusion

Band heaters melt the plastic at precisely controlled temperatures (160–210°C depending on polymer type). A single-screw extruder homogeneously mixes molten plastic with sand at optimal ratios (30–40% plastic).

03 Hydraulic Molding & Compaction

A hydraulic press compacts the molten composite into an interlocking tile mold with deterministic force. A rack-and-pinion sliding die system ensures precision positioning and ejection.

04 PLC Automation & Control

A Wecon PLC orchestrates the entire sequence — controlling the VFD-driven extruder motor, temperature PID controllers, hydraulic valves, proximity sensors, limit switches, and emergency stop logic.

Key Automation Components

Wecon LX3V-1212MR-A PLC Fuji FRENIC 5000G11 11kW VFD CHD402 PID Temperature Controller K-Type & J-Type Thermocouples Inductive Proximity Sensors Hydraulic Pressure Control Valves Solid State Relays (SSR-40 DA) Mechanical Limit Switches Emergency Stop (E-STOP) Band Heaters EPLAN Electrical Documentation SolidWorks 3D CAD Modeling

🧱 Output Tile Performance vs. Traditional Concrete

Property MYM Plastic–Sand Tile Traditional Concrete
Density 1200–1700 kg/m³ 2200–2400 kg/m³
Water Absorption <1–2% 6–8%
Impact Resistance High Moderate
Chemical Resistance Excellent Moderate
Carbon Footprint 50–70% lower High (cement kilns)
Raw Materials Recycled plastic + sand Cement + aggregates

📸 The Real Machine

The complete MYM Eco-Plastic Tile Machine — assembled and operational at Delta Technological University

Control panel internals (PLC, VFD, SSRs, MCBs) — and the final interlocking tiles produced by the machine

⚙️ What you see inside the panel

Wecon PLC, Fuji VFD (11 kW), MCBs, contactors, SSRs, PID temperature controllers, power transformer, and full DIN-rail wiring — all designed and wired by the team.

🧱 The tiles on top

The hexagonal MYM-branded interlocking tiles sitting on top of the control panel — the machine's final output, made entirely from recycled plastic and sand.

🌍 Sustainability & Impact

🇪🇬 Egypt Vision 2030

Supports economic self-reliance by replacing imported machinery with a locally manufactured mechatronic solution, reducing plastic pollution and conserving foreign currency.

🌐 UN SDGs Alignment

Directly contributes to SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation & Infrastructure) and SDG 12 (Responsible Consumption & Production) through circular economy principles.
